ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 represents black tea beverage as a closest parent; no specific code exists for the Singaporean Teh O Kosong preparation.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers tea infusion as a category; no specific code for unsweetened Singaporean-style black tea.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34728003 represents tea as a substance; used as closest parent for this plain black tea beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of plain black tea (approximately 240 ml / 240 g), served without sugar or milk as is typical for Teh O Kosong in Singapore kopitiam settings.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 14355)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea prepared with tap water is an exact match for Teh O Kosong — plain black tea brewed from tea leaves with water, no sugar, no milk. Values per 100 g of beverage.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — plain black tea contains no glycemic carbohydrates; GI set to 1 by convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain black tea (Teh O Kosong) contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and therefore has no measurable glycemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a numeric GI to zero-carbohydrate beverages. Per Project Veritas v2 zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) and gi_category is set to 'low'. Glycemic load per 240 g serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (3 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Teh O Kosong is suitable for individuals with hypertension as it contains virtually no sodium and no added salt; regular consumption of plain black tea has been associated with modest reductions in systolic blood pressure in some clinical studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teh O Kosong contains no sugar or digestible carbohydrate and will not raise postprandial blood glucose; it is a safe beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, provided no sugar or sweetened condensed milk is added.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of tea polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain black tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides flavonoid antioxidants (theaflavins, thearubigins) that have been associated with improved endothelial function and reduced LDL oxidation in cardiovascular research.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh O Kosong, also known as Tea Kosong or plain black tea, is a traditional Singaporean kopitiam beverage consisting solely of brewed black tea and water, served without sugar or milk. The name derives from Hokkien and Malay: 'Teh' means tea, 'O' indicates black (no milk), and 'Kosong' means empty or zero (no sugar). It is prepared by steeping robust black tea leaves — typically a blend of Assam and Ceylon — in hot water, then served either hot or over ice. The resulting beverage is amber to dark brown in colour with a slightly astringent, mildly bitter flavour and a clean, light body. Nutritionally, Teh O Kosong is essentially calorie-free at approximately 1 kcal per 100 g, with zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrate. It contains trace amounts of potassium (37 mg/100 g), magnesium (3 mg/100 g), and riboflavin, as well as naturally occurring caffeine and tea polyphenols including theaflavins and thearubigins. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and individuals following halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets. Clinically, it is an excellent beverage choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease, as it contributes no glycemic load, negligible sodium, and no saturated fat. It should not be confused with Teh O (which may contain sugar) or Teh Tarik (which contains condensed milk and sugar).
common_user_queries: Is plain black tea suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in Teh O Kosong?
What is the difference between Teh O and Teh O Kosong?
Does plain kopitiam tea have sugar?
Is Tea Kosong good for weight loss?
What is the GI of plain black tea?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for brewed black tea prepared with tap water, which is an exact compositional match for Teh O Kosong (plain black tea, no sugar, no milk). GI convention applied per Project Veritas v2 zero-calorie beverage rule. Multilingual names verified against standard Singaporean kopitiam terminology.
notes: energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g (trace energy from tea solids); this is effectively negligible but not set to zero as USDA reports 1 kcal. protein_g is naturally zero as plain brewed black tea contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as plain brewed black tea contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as plain brewed black tea contains no digestible carbohydrate (no sugar added). sugar_g is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for a brewed liquid beverage. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas v2 zero-calorie beverage glycemic convention. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for brewed black tea. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, and selenium_mcg are 0.0 — confirmed zero or below detection threshold per USDA FoodData Central. Input cuisine tag 'Italian' was incorrect; corrected to 'Singaporean' based on the food's established cultural origin as a kopitiam beverage. The field dataed (The Light Cafe — High Street Plaza) is a Singapore café; cuisine correction is fully supported. Price data from field survey (SGD 2.00) is preserved as contextual metadata only and does not affect nutritional values.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected cuisine from erroneous 'Italian' to 'Singaporean' based on food cultural origin.
Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Applied zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule: glycemic_index=1, gi_category='low', glycemic_load_per_serving=0.0.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g, sugar_g, dietary_fibre_g.
Sourced nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (brewed black tea, exact match).
Populated comprehensive aliases_by_language and verified consistency with flat aliases list.
Added specific clinical guidance for all three health conditions.
Wrote LLM-ready natural language description exceeding 200 characters.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: exact, closest_parent, none.
Documented all sentinel and zero values in notes per G3 and G4 requirements.
